ending: Ashfallen wins the Lunarshade war. Albus and Dorian are dead, the immediate Lunarshade bloodline is eliminated, and Tiberius orders the Eternal Pursuit Pavilion to treat Ashfallen cautiously and seek Stella as its contact. The cost is substantial: Chaos and Zeus are destroyed, Titus and Geb are badly damaged, Larry and Kaida are depleted, and Willow survives only through Ashlock's preserved soul shard. Willow is later restored as a Skyborne Bastion. Ashlock absorbs the remaining elemental Star Cores and passes the 10,000-credit threshold, then recovers before beginning his seven-day inner-world ascension. Stella, Diana, Elaine, Douglas, Larry, Kaida, the Redclaw family, Evelyn, Quill, Jasmine, and the city network remain aligned with him. Contact with a likely World Tree root suggests that the World Tree is in severe pain, but does not confirm Stella's claimed parentage or the cause. Nox begins a Bastion tribulation as Ashlock's ascension starts, and Vincent Nightrose is already approaching. The future beast tide, the World Tree's condition, Jasmine's path, and Ashfallen's expansion remain unresolved.
in_short: Ashlock expands his hidden root network, strengthens his summons, and builds Ashfallen into a wider military, commercial, and civic power while Stella trains Jasmine and investigates claims about her parents. A Nightshade City pill venture becomes a Lunarshade ambush when Seth is exposed as Albus Lunarshade. Ashlock answers with open war. Ashfallen's allies destroy Albus and the Lunarshade elders, then overcome Grand Elder Dorian after he ruins several Ents and Willow's Bastion. The victory gives Ashlock the sacrifices and elemental Star Cores required for Nascent Soul. After recovering, he reaches a likely root of the World Tree and learns only that it appears to be suffering. Willow is restored, Nox receives a new Bastion, and Ashlock begins his seven-day ascension as Vincent Nightrose moves to stop him.

recaps.text: Previously: Ashlock, a demonic tree and the hidden power behind Ashfallen, reached the ninth stage of the Star Core Realm. Stella, his adopted daughter and public agent, fights with spatial power alongside the half-demon Diana. Their allies include the researcher Elaine, the Mudcloak leader Douglas, the spider summon Larry, the ink-bodied lindworm Kaida, the Redclaw family, and several Ent defenders. Elder Mo serves the alliance as both cultivator and smith, while the Redclaw Grand Elder leads his family. Maple, a powerful World Walker, is also aligned with Ashlock but follows his own judgment. Ashfallen has grown around Red Vine Peak, with Stella acting openly for a ruler whose true nature remains concealed from most outsiders. Ashlock's immobile body and demanding route to Nascent Soul leave the sect dependent on careful alliances, cultivation resources, transport, and layered defenses against stronger powers. Its established members enter this volume as a coordinated but still developing force rather than a conventional sect.

Ashlock upgrades his roots with a captured Void key, gaining concealed routes that cross terrain and compress travel across his domain. He also approves Larry's divine evolution while reviewing the sacrifices and five elemental Star Cores still needed for Nascent Soul. Douglas resettles rescued Mudcloaks, and Ashfallen's leading cultivators report major gains. During a duel, Diana's demonic power becomes berserk while Stella reaches an unfamiliar bloodline state. Stella encounters a library of ancestral knowledge and an unidentified cosmic figure that claims the World Tree is her mother, her father is alive, and Ashlock will destroy the World Tree. The contact overwhelms her. Afterward, Ashlock, Stella, and Diana agree that none of these claims are proven. Directly approaching the Monarch Realm World Tree would be too dangerous, so they choose to investigate Stella's father instead. Stella and Diana will sell pills through the Eternal Pursuit Pavilion, using the proceeds to fund information and bounties for the elemental Star Cores Ashlock requires.

In Ashfallen City, the former Lunarshade merchant Knox survives as a shadow tree grown from a cursed soul fragment. Her separate shadow avatar frightens a nearby family, but Julian confirms it actually saved him after he exhausted his ki protecting Catherine and Jasmine. Ashlock places Knox under his control, appoints her provisional city guardian, and makes Julian the mortal trading branch's logistics head. Catherine and Jasmine enter Ashfallen. Jasmine chooses discipleship under Stella instead of an employment oath, forming a heaven-recognized bond, while Catherine swears loyalty. Ashlock identifies nature as Jasmine's strongest affinity and possible dual cultivation as an open choice. Elaine explains the difficulties of balancing two affinities, and Stella accepts that she must rely on specialists while teaching. Elsewhere, Sebastian Silverspire reveals preparations against Vincent Nightrose and asks to join Ashfallen's inner circle with Ryker. Ashlock offers them vassal status instead. A recruitment tournament is planned, with Stella delaying it so Jasmine can train. Jasmine then begins formal nature cultivation in a purpose-built hut on Red Vine Peak, still without choosing a second affinity.

Ashlock completes a concealed transport hub linking Ashfallen's major settlements and turns routes into Diana's demonic-mist grove into a trap for wandering monsters. Larry emerges from his evolution as an S-grade divine-ash herald with authority over decay, the ability to reform from ash, and enough independence to make his own choices while remaining loyal. Elaine warns that an exceptional beast tide may reach the region in two or three years, possibly ending with Nascent Soul monsters. Since Ashlock cannot relocate, she proposes extending his roots toward the northern spiritual spring, killing early waves, and remaking suitable corpses into Ents before stronger monsters arrive. Jasmine's training intensifies. She overcomes an illusion built from her fear of Stella, earns Elder Mo's teaching sword, and discovers that unarmed combat may suit her better. Ashlock then approaches Nightshade City. Knox proposes using her sister as a pill-selling proxy because direct action could attract the city's powerful warden. Stella and Diana enter the Eternal Pursuit Pavilion. Diana seeks promotion, while Stella registers under her real name and finds an outdated bounty on herself. Her name causes an informant to alert others, creating an immediate exposure risk.

Stella becomes an Iron Seeker and learns that another person has registered under the Ashfallen bloodline. Seth, another Iron Seeker, offers her and Diana a share in the bounty on a Lunarshade target. Diana's promotion duel pits her against a Mistrout cultivator whose Mystic Key summon is actually a World Walker exploiting the ritual. The entity identifies itself as Maple's younger sibling, bargains for a brief fight, and withdraws after Diana fulfills the terms. Diana wins promotion as the Crimson Tracker Raven. Maple later confirms the relationship, and Stella names the entity Pluto. Stella and Diana then confront Evelyn Duskwalker, Nox's sister and acting head of a weakened household. Backed by Larry and Ashlock, they compel Evelyn and three allied elders to accept a binding proxy contract. Ashfallen will provide protection and rare pills, while the Duskwalkers sell them and transfer most proceeds. Evelyn reveals that the bounty target is her abusive Lunarshade husband. A truffle removes Evelyn's heart demon and lets her reach the third stage of Star Core. Her allies recognize her as temporary Grand Elder, and she brings the group to her cousin Mark for the first pavilion pill evaluations.

Ashlock completes a wind-affinity mission, absorbs the slain wyvern's Star Core, and unintentionally inspires worship as the All-Seeing Gaze. He learns that disorder in Ashfallen City includes gangs, kidnappings, and rogue cultivators, so the sect plans mortal government, guards, employment, schooling, and limited cultivation access. Stella takes Jasmine into the mines to eliminate the rogues. She frees their prisoners and leaves one cultivator alive for Jasmine's training. Jasmine drains Earth key from him and deliberately avoids killing him, reaching the fourth Key Realm stage but revealing a continuing conflict between compassion and Stella's expectations. Ashlock kills the released rogue after Stella honors her promise of freedom. In the pavilion, Ashlock gains a hidden root route and formalizes the growing All-Seeing Eye cult. Stella becomes its leader, Larry its herald, and Elysia its vice leader. Stella and Diana then follow Seth to hunt Albus Lunarshade despite strong signs of a trap. At a Lunarshade gambling house, Stella claims the title First Princess of Ashfallen to provoke a bloodline response. An ancestral finger technique lets her kill the guard commander, and the alarm begins the battle.

Stella's new finger technique nearly drains her dry, allowing a Lunarshade guard to wound and immobilize her. Ashlock intervenes with his Ents and discovers that Seth is actually Albus Lunarshade, while the pavilion's Grand Elder is Albus's father. Chaos kills Albus, but the Lunarshade Grand Elder destroys Chaos and Zeus and badly damages Titus. Larry contests the enemy's moonlight soul avatar while Willow's Skyborne Bastion pins it beneath a spatial shield. The conflict expands when the Eternal Pursuit Pavilion offers a rich bounty for protecting the Grand Elder. Douglas and Elaine join the defense aboard Geb, a mobile fortress built by the Mudcloaks, and Douglas accepts their kingship. The Redclaw Grand Elder resumes the name Magnus and leads Elder Margret, Elder Mo, and Amber into the war. Geb's cannon wounds Lunarshade elders but disrupts Willow's containment, freeing the enemy avatar. Ashlock summons the All-Seeing Eye cult and the bound Nightshade families, then converts fallen Lunarshade elders into Ents. Their followers interpret this as divine action. Collective worship causes Heaven to recognize Ashlock as a deity and bless Red Vine Peak, even though the Lunarshade force remains dangerous and Geb's defenses are failing.

Ashfallen's allies begin reducing the Lunarshade elders, but one teleports into Geb's control room. Elaine uses void movement and illusion to make the attacker believe moonlight is burning him, allowing Douglas to kill him before Ashlock evacuates the Mudcloaks. Grand Elder Dorian then spends life force on a devastating moonbeam. He depletes Larry, destroys Geb's head, and shatters Willow's Bastion. The destruction releases Ashlock's cursed blood into Dorian's avatar, corrupting him from within. Diana lures more elders into a trap where Larry consumes them, while Stella and Diana withdraw wounded. Kaida expends his inscribed scales in a combined attack that cripples Dorian. Larry delivers him to Ashlock, who stops his final detonation and kills him after the branch families have been eradicated. Celestial Warden Tiberius orders the pavilion to treat Ashfallen as a possible divine threat and seek contact through Stella. The victory is costly: Chaos and Zeus are gone, Titus and Geb are maimed, Larry and Kaida are depleted, and Willow is badly damaged but alive through Ashlock's soul shard. By consuming the fallen, Ashlock obtains the missing elemental Star Cores and surpasses the 10,000 sacrificial credits required for Nascent Soul.

Ashlock delays ascension because his damaged soul, low reserves, and the week-long process would leave him exposed to Vincent Nightrose. He sleeps for three days, fully recovers, creates lunar Ent guards, and sends his roots downward to a ley line. Stella begins teaching Jasmine to use nature affinity to reach and heal Willow. Ashlock discovers that the ley line is likely a root of the World Tree and touches its golden sap and divine power, drawing the attention of a Monarch Realm consciousness. The resulting pressure attacks his entire root network until his mental defenses improve. Stella senses agony rather than a deliberate attack, leading Ashlock to suspect that the World Tree is suffering, though neither imprisonment nor Stella's claimed kinship is proven. Ashfallen commits to becoming strong enough to help it. Ashlock restores Willow as a Skyborne Bastion and appoints Nox Duskwalker to guard Ashfallen City through a new onyx-shadow Bastion. Nox's transformation triggers a tribulation. Ashlock then begins forming his inner world. His seven-day Nascent Soul ascension reaches one percent as heavenly tribulation threatens him and his offspring, while Vincent senses the event and moves to intervene.
